channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: He recorded his quest for tariff refunds. It shows why billions may never get repaid
item.description: After the Supreme Court struck down most of President Trump's tariffs, Richard Brown began lining up the paperwork he needed to get his refund. Experts say many businesses may never get their money back.
item.content:encoded:
After the Supreme Court struck down most of President Trump's tariffs, Richard Brown began lining up the paperwork he needed to get his refund. Experts say many businesses may never get their money back.

(Image credit: Daniel Lozada for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: Why this tribe is buying up hundreds of acres of farmland — and flooding it
item.description: The Stillaguamish Tribe in Washington state has been buying land in its traditional territory and removing levees. The goal is to turn farmland into wetlands with the hopes of restoring Chinook salmon.
item.content:encoded:
The Stillaguamish Tribe in Washington state has been buying land in its traditional territory and removing levees. The goal is to turn farmland into wetlands with the hopes of restoring Chinook salmon.

(Image credit: Megan Farmer /KUOW)
item.dc:creator: John Ryan

item.title: How algorithms wreaked havoc with these workers' schedules and cut their pay
item.description: Hourly workers across a number of industries have long been grappling with unstable schedules and pay as their employers use software to slash labor costs and maximize productivity.
item.content:encoded:
Hourly workers across a number of industries have long been grappling with unstable schedules and pay as their employers use software to slash labor costs and maximize productivity.

(Image credit: Huo Jingnan)
item.dc:creator: Huo Jingnan

item.title: Germany says U.S. troop withdrawal 'anticipated', Spain and Italy could be next
item.description: Germany's defense minister is playing down the impact of the Pentagon's decision to pull 5,000 troops from the country, but the move has rattled NATO allies and added to growing fears that Europe can no longer rely on Washington.
item.content:encoded:
Germany's defense minister is playing down the impact of the Pentagon's decision to pull 5,000 troops from the country, but the move has rattled NATO allies and added to growing fears that Europe can no longer rely on Washington.

(Image credit: Markus Schreiber)
item.dc:creator: Willem Marx

item.title: Bard College's president to retire after scrutiny of relationship with Jeffrey Epstein
item.description: The longtime president of Bard College in New York has announced his retirement, months after it was revealed that he had a much deeper relationship than was previously known with Jeffrey Epstein.
item.content:encoded:
The longtime president of Bard College in New York has announced his retirement, months after it was revealed that he had a much deeper relationship than was previously known with Jeffrey Epstein.

(Image credit: Philip Kamrass)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Golden Tempo takes the Kentucky Derby as Cherie DeVaux becomes the 1st woman to train its winner
item.description: Golden Tempo has won the Kentucky Derby at odds of 23-1 to make Cherie DeVaux the first woman to train the winner of the opening leg of the Triple Crown.
item.content:encoded:
Golden Tempo has won the Kentucky Derby at odds of 23-1 to make Cherie DeVaux the first woman to train the winner of the opening leg of the Triple Crown.

(Image credit: Jeff Roberson)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Iran submits 14-point response to U.S. proposal to end war
item.description: Iran has presented a 14-point response to the U.S. proposal to end the war, according to Iranian state media.
item.content:encoded:
Iran has presented a 14-point response to the U.S. proposal to end the war, according to Iranian state media.

(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Timmy the stranded whale rescued after weekslong effort
item.description: Timmy captured the hearts of whale lovers across the globe who rooted for a happy ending for the humpback.
item.content:encoded:
Timmy captured the hearts of whale lovers across the globe who rooted for a happy ending for the humpback.

(Image credit: Sebastian Peters)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise

item.title: Academy announces major overhaul to rules
item.description: The new rules focus on areas such as AI protections for writers and actors and expanded eligibility for international films.
item.content:encoded:
The new rules focus on areas such as AI protections for writers and actors and expanded eligibility for international films.

(Image credit: Frederic J. Brown)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: Taiwan's Lai lands in Eswatini in a trip delayed by lack of overflight clearance
item.description: Eswatini remains the only African nation without tariff-free access to China's market due to its ties with Taiwan.
item.content:encoded:
Eswatini remains the only African nation without tariff-free access to China's market due to its ties with Taiwan.

(Image credit: ChiangYing-ying)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
